description: Bird size was slightly larger than the myriad of ring-billed gulls milling around, but smaller than the Herring Gulls and the Glaucous Gull seen in the vicinity.  Bill was completely dark.  Head and body had very faint brown pattern.  The bird almost appeared like a dirty white.  Primaries also appeared to have a very light brown coloring.  I compared the primary feather color of the bird to the observed glaucous primaries and the glaucous was a clear white while the iceland had a dirty white color.  Legs were pink.  I couldn't get a good view of the eye color or more detailed looks at feather color pattern on the wings given the distance I was viewing from.  The closest other possibility was a Thayer's which I eliminated based on the almost white coloring of the bird.  Glaucous-winged was an extremely remote possibility, but the smaller size, overall light coloring, and lack of a larger bill eliminated this option.
